title: CRDs, Controllers, Operators, Admission Policies, Webhooks, and Extension Trust · API and Operations Recall
summary: An on-call handoff reconstructs crds, controllers, operators, admission policies, webhooks, and extension trust from delayed object status, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the trusted extension API and controller contract under changed capacity. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for trusted extension API and controller contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Design CRD names, scope, versions, structural OpenAPI schemas, defaulting, validation, pruning, status subresource, scale, storage version, and conversion.
Build or evaluate controllers from watches, work queues, level-based reconciliation, ownership, idempotency, finalizers, status, retries, leader election, and rate limits.
Choose built-in admission, ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, validating webhook, or mutating webhook from expressiveness, failure policy, ordering, timeout, availability, and audit needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion · changed case · Review K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion
steps.instruction:
review case — A review CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall.

Which action demonstrates K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion · changed case · Review K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design crd names, scope, versions, structural openapi schemas, defaulting, validation, pruning, status subresource, scale, storage version, and conversion. For K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion · changed case · Review K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion, keep K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

At K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion · changed case · Review K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A review CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall.

For K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion · changed case · Review K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A CRD automatically supplies a controller and preserves unknown fields and old versions forever.

options.text: A CRD automatically supplies a controller and preserves unknown fields and old versions forever. That claim fails the k8s crd schema status conversion · changed case evidence boundary for K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion.

For k8s crd schema status conversion · changed case, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof. This does not satisfy K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion for trusted extension API and controller contract.

Design CRD names, scope, versions, structural OpenAPI schemas, defaulting, validation, pruning, status subresource, scale, storage version, and conversion. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for trusted extension API and controller contract.
